title: Protocol: Nigeria's trial deficit against large developing-country comparators
primary_estimand: Nigeria gap ratio: comparator per-capita trial rate divided by Nigeria per-capita rate
body: This cross-sectional registry study will quantify Nigeria clinical trial deficit relative to comparator large developing countries and dissect its trial portfolio structure. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov for all interventional trials located in Nigeria and in India, Indonesia, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Ethiopia, and Brazil, computing per-capita rates and gap ratios for each pair. The primary outcome is the Nigeria gap ratio for each comparator, defined as comparator per-capita trial rate divided by Nigeria per-capita rate. Secondary outcomes include condition breakdown across twelve disease categories, sickle cell disease trial count relative to burden of approximately 150,000 affected births per year, sponsor classification as local versus foreign, phase distribution, completion versus termination rates, and geographic concentration across Nigerian cities. Classification rules for sponsor origin will use keyword matching against known Nigerian institutions and will be specified before data extraction begins. No patient-level data will be used. The analysis is limited to a single registry and cannot capture locally registered studies.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.value: Nigeria gap ratio = comparator per-capita rate / Nigeria per-capita rate
condition breakdown (12 diseases), SCD count, sponsor local/foreign, phase, status, city concentration
India, Indonesia, Bangladesh, Pakistan, Ethiopia, Brazil
keyword matching against known Nigerian universities, hospitals, federal centers
